claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my son, Alex. He's 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 16 years old, his eligibility depends on what he's currently doing. Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in full-time education or training, or has he recently left education?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in education. He's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that. Studying A-levels at sixth form college counts as approved education, so that's helpful for Alex's eligibility.

Now I just need to check a few more things about your circumstances:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

**Does Alex live with you, or does he live elsewhere?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is Alex working 24 hours or more per week, or receiving any benefits in his own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working that much - he doesn't have a job at all actually, he's just focused on his studies. And he's not receiving any benefits in his own name either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to tell you that **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

✅ **You live in the UK** (Manchester)

✅ **Alex lives with you**, meaning you're responsible for him

✅ **Alex is in approved education** – studying A-levels at sixth form college qualifies as approved education, which allows Child Benefit to continue for children aged 16-19

✅ **No one else is claiming** Child Benefit for Alex

✅ **Alex is not working 24+ hours per week** and is not receiving benefits in his own name

You can continue to receive Child Benefit for Alex until he either finishes his approved education/training, turns 20 (whichever comes first), or his circumstances change (such as starting full-time work of 24+ hours per week or receiving certain benefits).

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old, which is above the standard age limit of under 16. However, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. Additionally: (1) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (2) Alex lives at home with the claimant, meaning the claimant is responsible for him, (3) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (4) Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week, and (5) Alex is not receiving any benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They live in the UK, Alex lives with them, Alex is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), no one else is claiming for Alex, and Alex is not working excessive hours or receiving benefits in his own name. All the standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.

However, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 if they remain in approved education or training. Alex is currently studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. Additionally: (1) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (2) Alex lives at home with the claimant, meaning the claimant is responsible for him, (3) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (4) Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week, and (5) Alex is not receiving any benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They live in the UK, Alex lives with them, Alex is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), no one else is claiming for Alex, and Alex is not working excessive hours or receiving benefits in his own name. All the standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.
